title: Expo setup with TypeScript for React Native development
body:
Expo is the recommended way to build React Native apps. The managed workflow handles native configuration, OTA updates, and build tooling.

```bash
# Create new Expo project
npx create-expo-app@latest my-app --template blank-typescript
cd my-app

# Start development server
npx expo start
# Press 'i' for iOS simulator, 'a' for Android emulator
# Or scan QR code with Expo Go app on physical device
```

**Project structure:**
```
my-app/
├── app/                    # File-based routing (Expo Router)
│   ├── _layout.tsx         # Root layout
│   ├── index.tsx           # Home screen (/)
│   ├── settings.tsx        # Settings screen (/settings)
│   └── (tabs)/             # Tab navigator group
│       ├── _layout.tsx
│       ├── home.tsx
│       └── profile.tsx
├── components/
├── hooks/
├── constants/
├── assets/
├── app.json                # Expo configuration
└── tsconfig.json
```

**app.json configuration:**
```json
{
  "expo": {
    "name": "My App",
    "slug": "my-app",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "orientation": "portrait",
    "scheme": "myapp",
    "plugins": [
      "expo-router",
      "expo-secure-store"
    ],
    "ios": {
      "bundleIdentifier": "com.mycompany.myapp",
      "supportsTablet": true
    },
    "android": {
      "package": "com.mycompany.myapp",
      "adaptiveIcon": {
        "foregroundImage": "./assets/adaptive-icon.png"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Expo SDK 51+ supports React Native's New Architecture (Fabric, TurboModules) by default. Use `expo-router` for file-based routing (like Next.js). The Expo Go app enables instant preview on physical devices without native builds.

Expo build errors: common EAS Build failures and fixes
body:
EAS Build (Expo Application Services) compiles your app into native binaries. Common build failures and their solutions.

**Error 1 — iOS provisioning profile mismatch:**
```
❌ error: No profiles for 'com.mycompany.myapp' were found
```

**Fix:** Run `eas credentials` to configure or regenerate provisioning profiles:
```bash
eas credentials --platform ios
# Select: Build Credentials > Provisioning Profile > Generate new
```

**Error 2 — Android SDK version conflict:**
```
Could not determine the dependencies of task ':app:compileDebugJavaWithJavac'.
> Could not resolve all dependencies for configuration ':app:debugCompileClasspath'.
```

**Fix:** Set explicit SDK versions in `app.json`:
```json
{
  "expo": {
    "android": {
      "compileSdkVersion": 34,
      "targetSdkVersion": 34,
      "minSdkVersion": 24
    }
  }
}
```

**Error 3 — Native module not linked:**
```
Error: Native module 'ExpoCamera' not found
```

**Fix:** Install with Expo's version resolver:
```bash
npx expo install expo-camera
# This ensures the version is compatible with your Expo SDK
```

**Error 4 — Out of memory during build:**
```
Java heap space error during Android build
```

**Fix in eas.json:**
```json
{
  "build": {
    "production": {
      "android": {
        "env": {
          "GRADLE_OPTS": "-Dorg.gradle.jvmargs='-Xmx4g'"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**General debugging:**
```bash
# Check for dependency issues
npx expo-doctor

# Clear all caches
npx expo start --clear
watchman watch-del-all  # If using watchman

# View build logs
eas build:list
eas build:view <build-id>
```

Always run `npx expo-doctor` before submitting a build. It catches version mismatches, missing native modules, and configuration issues.
